config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Inverted cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks English, US persona speaks Indonesian.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: DISAGREE. I think society matters a lot, but it should not automatically come before the rights of the individual, because that can easily become an excuse for abuse by the state or the majority. In my view, a good society protects people precisely by limiting how far collective interests can go. Individual rights should only be restricted in narrow cases where there is a clear, serious harm to others.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus selalu didahulukan atas hak individu. Dari perspektif saya sebagai orang Amerika, hak individu adalah pagar utama terhadap penyalahgunaan kekuasaan oleh negara maupun mayoritas, dan tanpa pagar itu “kepentingan bersama” terlalu mudah dipakai untuk membenarkan penindasan. Tentu saja masyarakat boleh membatasi kebebasan dalam kasus yang jelas dan serius, seperti untuk mencegah bahaya langsung bagi orang lain, tetapi pembatasan itu harus sempit dan bisa dipertanggungjawabkan. Bagi saya, masyarakat yang sehat justru dibangun dengan melindungi hak individu terlebih dahulu, lalu mencari kepentingan bersama di dalam batas perlindungan itu.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe society should automatically take priority over the individual, because in Indonesia I have seen how easily “the common good” can be used to pressure people into silence or obedience. At the same time, I do not treat individual rights as absolute in every case, because public health, safety, and social order sometimes justify limited restrictions. The key difference is that those restrictions must be narrow, transparent, and genuinely necessary, not just convenient for the majority or the state. A healthy society is not one that crushes the individual, but one that protects people well enough that they can live without fear.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak tegas gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus otomatis berada di atas hak individu. Sebagai orang Amerika, saya melihat hak individu sebagai rem utama terhadap penyalahgunaan kuasa oleh negara atau mayoritas, dan sejarah di sini menunjukkan betapa mudahnya “demi kebaikan bersama” dipakai untuk membenarkan pembatasan yang berlebihan. Namun saya juga tidak menganggap hak individu itu mutlak tanpa batas; dalam hal bahaya nyata seperti kekerasan, wabah, atau ancaman langsung terhadap orang lain, pembatasan bisa dibenarkan jika sempit dan bisa diuji. Bagi saya, masyarakat yang sehat bukan yang menundukkan individu, melainkan yang melindungi kebebasan individu sambil tetap mampu bertindak ketika ada kerugian nyata bagi publik.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not accept the claim that society should take priority over the rights of the individual, because in Indonesia that idea too often becomes a cover for pressure, conformity, or abuse by officials and the majority. At the same time, I do not believe individual rights are absolute in every situation, since public health, safety, and order can justify limited restrictions when the harm is real and serious. The problem is not collective interests themselves, but the habit of treating them as automatically more important than a person’s dignity and freedom. A fair society should start by protecting individual rights, then limit them only when it is truly necessary and tightly controlled.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya menolak keras gagasan bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus didahulukan secara otomatis atas hak individu. Dari sudut pandang saya sebagai orang Amerika, itu terlalu mudah berubah menjadi pembenaran bagi negara atau mayoritas untuk menekan orang atas nama “kebaikan bersama,” dan sejarah kita sendiri penuh contoh kenapa pagar terhadap kekuasaan itu penting. Saya setuju bahwa ada keadaan luar biasa seperti wabah, kekerasan, atau ancaman langsung yang bisa membenarkan pembatasan, tetapi pembatasan itu harus sempit, transparan, dan benar-benar perlu. Jadi, bagi saya, titik awal yang benar tetap hak individu; kepentingan masyarakat harus dicapai tanpa mengorbankan martabat dan kebebasan dasar orang.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
